sections.paragraph:
f. once מִלְחֶמֶת 1Sa 13:22 with suff. מִלְחַמְתֹּו, plur. מִלְחָמֹות (from the root לָחַם No. 2).

(1) pr. inf. or abstract verbal noun; to fight, a fighting. Isa 7:1, לַמִּלְחָמָה עָלֶיהָ “to fight against it” (the city), to besiege it. Hence battle, Exo 13:17 Job 39:25.

(2) war, עָשָׂה מִלְחָמָה, followed by אֵת (אִתּ) and עִם to wage war against any one, Gen 14:2 Deu 20:12, 20 Deu 20:20. הָֽיְתָה מִלְחָמָה בְּ there was war with any one, 2Sa 21:15, 20 2Sa 21:20. אִישׁ מִלְחָמָה a warrior, a soldier, Num 31:28 אִישׁ מִלְחָמֹות id. Isa 42:13 also an adversary in war, 1Ch 18:10. עַם מִלְחָמָה das Kriegsvolk, army, Jos 8:1 11:7 . Meton. i.q. כְּלֵי מִלְחָמָה instruments of war, arms, Psa 76:4 compare Hos 1:7 2:20 .

(3) victory, the fortune of war (pr. the devouring of enemies), Ecc 9:11, לֹא לִגִּבֹּרִים מִלְחָמָה “victory (does) not (always happen) to the strong.”
